name: Hou Yi Full
description: Hou Yi, also known as Shen Yi, is a legendary archer in Chinese mythology. He is often depicted as a divine figure who descended from Heaven to aid humanity. During the reign of Emperor Yao, ten suns appeared in the sky, scorching the earth and causing immense suffering. Crops withered, rivers dried up, and people faced starvation. Emperor Yao, unable to stop the catastrophe, pleaded for divine intervention. The Jade Emperor, observing the plight of humanity, sent Hou Yi down to Earth with his wife, Chang'e. Hou Yi, armed with his celestial bow and arrows, embarked on a mission to save the world. He skillfully shot down nine of the ten suns, leaving only one to provide light and warmth. This act restored balance to the world and earned him the gratitude of humankind. Following his heroic feat, Hou Yi sought the elixir of immortality from the Queen Mother of the West, hoping to become immortal with his wife. However, Chang'e consumed the elixir herself, leading to her ascension to the moon, where she became the Moon Goddess. Hou Yi's story is a testament to his courage, skill, and selflessness, solidifying his place as a revered figure in Chinese mythology. He is celebrated for his ability to overcome seemingly insurmountable challenges and his dedication to protecting humanity from destruction. His tale is often associated with themes of balance, sacrifice, and the consequences of ambition.
subtitle: The Divine Archer Who Saved the World
achievements: Saved the world from scorching heat

keyMyths.title: Shooting Down the Suns
keyMyths.description: Hou Yi was tasked with shooting down the ten suns that were scorching the earth. He successfully shot down nine, leaving one to provide light and warmth, saving humanity from a fiery death.

keyMyths.title: Chang'e's Flight to the Moon
keyMyths.description: Hou Yi obtained the elixir of immortality, but Chang'e consumed it herself, causing her to float to the moon and become the Moon Goddess. This event is a central part of the Mid-Autumn Festival.

keyMyths.title: Slaying the Monsters
keyMyths.description: Beyond shooting down the suns, Hou Yi also used his archery skills to defeat various monsters and beasts that plagued the land. He helped restore order and peace to the mortal realm.

sources.author: Yang Lihui
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Chinese mythological figures and stories.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Chinese mythological figures and stories.
